meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve no understanding of APIs (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching for Students: Master API Integration
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no API knowledge and professional freelance success. Follow this checklist to master integration using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop letting a lack of API knowledge stall your career. By leveraging Claude Code to generate robust boilerplate and Antigravity to abstract complex endpoints, you can pitch professional-grade integrations to clients today. Master these tools to turn technical gaps into high-value, billable features for your first freelance project.
content.intro:
You stare at the documentation, a labyrinth of JSON objects and authentication headers, feeling the familiar sting of imposter syndrome. You have the vision for the app, but the 'connective tissue'—the APIs—feels like a black box you aren't qualified to open. It is the silent killer of student projects and the primary reason freelance bids get rejected.

But here is the secret: you don't need to be a backend architect to ship. You need to be a master of orchestration. By pivoting your workflow to utilize intelligent CLI agents and abstraction layers, you can bypass the manual grind of request-handling and start delivering sophisticated integrations that command premium freelance rates.

steps.title: Audit the Client's API Requirements
steps.description: Before writing code, map out the data flow. Use Claude Code to analyze the documentation and identify the necessary authentication methods (OAuth, API Keys).
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided API documentation and generate a schema mapping the required endpoints to our application's state management needs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the API docs in /docs and output a JSON summary of authentication requirements.'

steps.title: Generate Secure Boilerplate
steps.description: Never write manual fetch requests. Use Claude Code to build a type-safe API client that handles errors and retries automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust API service layer in TypeScript using axios, including interceptors for token injection and error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a modular service layer for this API, ensuring all requests are wrapped in try-catch blocks.'

steps.title: Abstract Endpoint Logic with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to create a high-level abstraction so your frontend components don't need to know how the API works under the hood.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Encapsulate these API endpoints into a clean, promise-based hook library that exposes only the necessary data functions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the API service to expose custom React hooks for each endpoint.'

steps.title: Mocking for Rapid Prototyping
steps.description: Show clients progress before the backend is fully live. Generate mock data to demonstrate the UI/UX flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock server configuration that mirrors the API schema to allow for end-to-end testing without hitting live endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a mock server using MSW that replicates the production API response structure.'

steps.title: Pitch the 'Integration First' Strategy
steps.description: Package your technical work into a professional pitch. Present the integration not as a 'task,' but as a 'strategic data bridge' for the client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional pitch email for a prospective client highlighting our ability to integrate [API Name] securely and efficiently.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a project proposal focusing on the technical feasibility and security of the proposed API integration.'

pro_tips: Always wrap third-party API calls in an internal 'adapter' layer to make switching providers trivial.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API service layer *before* you connect to the real endpoint.
When pitching, offer a 'Proof of Concept' integration phase to lower the client's perceived risk.
Cache API responses locally using React Query or SWR to make your app feel instantaneous.
Document your API integration as a 'Client Handover Guide' to increase your perceived value and freelance credibility.
content.next_steps: Deploy your first integrated prototype to Vercel and create a 60-second Loom video demonstrating the API functionality to your first freelance prospect.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn technical hurdles into a professional portfolio. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' time to start. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
